summary:
The lowest risk complication with an outlet forceps delivery involves minor bleeding at the vaginal opening rather than severe tissue tears that might occur during such deliveries. This makes \boxed{B} the best choice for minimizing post-delivery complications. To improve this summary while adhering strictly to your instructions, here are two concise sentences focusing only on the key clinical point related to the question asked:

Vulvar hematomas from outlet forceps use typically do not result in major long-term problems as they involve minimal soft-tissue injury compared to other potential complications. Thus,
\boxed{B}. Vulvar hematoma represents the least complex issue associated with using outlet forceps.
